creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
/usr/bin/objdump is currently provided by obsolete GNU binutils 2.17.50, and is expectd to be removed by FreeBSD 13.0.  The various lang/*-gcc packages are one of three groups of packages that failed to build in an exp-run [PR 212319].

Without /usr/bin/objdump the port builds fine but fails the package step. Error log:
=======================<phase: package        >============================
===>  Building package for powerpc64-gcc-6.4.0_6
pkg-static: Unable to access file /wrkdirs/usr/ports/devel/powerpc64-gcc/work/stage/usr/local/lib/gcc/powerpc64-unknown-freebsd13.0/6.4.0/plugin/gtype.state:No such file or directory
pkg-static: Unable to access file /wrkdirs/usr/ports/devel/powerpc64-gcc/work/stage/usr/local/lib/gcc/powerpc64-unknown-freebsd13.0/6.4.0/plugin/include/ada/gcc-interface/ada-tree.def:No such file or directory
pkg-static: Unable to access file /wrkdirs/usr/ports/devel/powerpc64-gcc/work/stage/usr/local/lib/gcc/powerpc64-unknown-freebsd13.0/6.4.0/plugin/include/addresses.h:No such file or directory
...

http://package22.nyi.freebsd.org/data/headamd64PR212319-default/2019-10-05_20h22m03s/logs/errors/powerpc64-gcc-6.4.0_6.log
